name: Swap To The Single-K 128x128 Non-Microkernel Sibling

summary.name: Swap To The Single-K 128x128 Non-Microkernel Sibling
summary.hypothesis: The grouped-row experiment showed that launch-order changes alone are not enough. The existing single-K 128x128 sibling keeps the accepted row split and one-K stage depth while changing the accumulate ordering away from the microkernel-specific path, which may reduce long-scoreboard waste directly.
summary.expected_bottleneck: microkernel-specific accumulate ordering is contributing to long-scoreboard stalls on the accepted hot-band split
summary.risk: Moderate risk: the variant is in-tree, but it still lacks a clean measurement on the accepted row split.
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest next experiment that directly targets the long-scoreboard gap exposed by the grouped-row result.
It preserves the accepted hot-band row split, so the measurement stays focused on one kernel-variant change.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if runtime regresses again while long-scoreboard stalls stay flat or worsen.
expected_local_changes: Restore the grouped-row constant back to the clean accepted base if needed.
Swap the default hot-band launch from the PTX microkernel path to the existing single-K non-microkernel 128x128 sibling.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
